NKJV.1: A Psalm of Asaph. God stands in the congregation of the mighty; He judges among the gods.
NKJV.2: How long will you judge unjustly, And show partiality to the wicked? Selah
NKJV.3: Defend the poor and fatherless; Do justice to the afflicted and needy.
NKJV.4: Deliver the poor and needy; Free them from the hand of the wicked.
NKJV.5: They do not know, nor do they understand; They walk about in darkness; All the foundations of the earth are unstable.
NKJV.6: I said, “You are gods, And all of you are children of the Most High.
NKJV.7: But you shall die like men, And fall like one of the princes.”
NKJV.8: Arise, O God, judge the earth;  For You shall inherit all nations.

KJV.1: A Psalm<S>4210</S> of Asaph<S>623</S>. God<S>430</S> standeth<S>5324</S> in the congregation<S>5712</S> of the mighty<S>410</S>; he judgeth<S>8199</S> among<S>7130</S> the gods<S>430</S>. <sup>of Asaph: or, for Asaph</sup>
KJV.2: How long will ye judge<S>8199</S> unjustly<S>5766</S>, and accept<S>5375</S> the persons<S>6440</S> of the wicked<S>7563</S>? Selah<S>5542</S>.
KJV.3: Defend<S>8199</S> the poor<S>1800</S> and fatherless<S>3490</S>: do justice<S>6663</S> to the afflicted<S>6041</S> and needy<S>7326</S>. <sup>Defend: Heb. Judge</sup>
KJV.4: Deliver<S>6403</S> the poor<S>1800</S> and needy<S>34</S>: rid<S>5337</S> them out of the hand<S>3027</S> of the wicked<S>7563</S>.
KJV.5: They know<S>3045</S> not, neither will they understand<S>995</S>; they walk<S>1980</S> on in darkness<S>2825</S>: all the foundations<S>4144</S> of the earth<S>776</S> are out of course<S>4131</S>. <sup>out: Heb. moved</sup>
KJV.6: I have said<S>559</S>, Ye are gods<S>430</S>; and all of you are children<S>1121</S> of the most High<S>5945</S>.
KJV.7: But<S>403</S> ye shall die<S>4191</S> like men<S>120</S>, and fall<S>5307</S> like one<S>259</S> of the princes<S>8269</S>.
KJV.8: Arise<S>6965</S>, O God<S>430</S>, judge<S>8199</S> the earth<S>776</S>: for thou shalt inherit<S>5157</S> all nations<S>1471</S>.

ESV.1: God has taken his place in the divine council; in the midst of the gods he holds judgment:
ESV.2: “How long will you judge unjustly and show partiality to the wicked? Selah
ESV.3: Give justice to the weak and the fatherless; maintain the right of the afflicted and the destitute.
ESV.4: Rescue the weak and the needy; deliver them from the hand of the wicked.”
ESV.5: They have neither knowledge nor understanding, they walk about in darkness; all the foundations of the earth are shaken.
ESV.6: I said, “You are gods, sons of the Most High, all of you;
ESV.7: nevertheless, like men you shall die, and fall like any prince.”
ESV.8: Arise, O God, judge the earth; for you shall inherit all the nations!

NASB.1: A Psalm of Asaph. God takes His stand in His own congregation; He judges in the midst of the rulers.
NASB.2: How long will you judge unjustly And show partiality to the wicked? Selah.
NASB.3: Vindicate the weak and fatherless; Do justice to the afflicted and destitute.
NASB.4: Rescue the weak and needy; Deliver [them] out of the hand of the wicked.
NASB.5: They do not know nor do they understand; They walk about in darkness; All the foundations of the earth are shaken.
NASB.6: I said, 'You are gods, And all of you are sons of the Most High.
NASB.7: 'Nevertheless you will die like men And fall like [any] one of the princes.'
NASB.8: Arise, O God, judge the earth! For it is You who possesses all the nations.

NLT.1:
God presides over heaven’s court;
he pronounces judgment on the heavenly beings:
NLT.2:
“How long will you hand down unjust decisions
by favoring the wicked? Interlude
NLT.3:
“Give justice to the poor and the orphan;
uphold the rights of the oppressed and the destitute.
NLT.4:
Rescue the poor and helpless;
deliver them from the grasp of evil people.
NLT.5:
But these oppressors know nothing;
they are so ignorant!
They wander about in darkness,
while the whole world is shaken to the core.
NLT.6:
I say, ‘You are gods;
you are all children of the Most High.
NLT.7:
But you will die like mere mortals
and fall like every other ruler.’”
NLT.8:
Rise up, O God, and judge the earth,
for all the nations belong to you.

WEB.1: A Psalm by Asaph. God presides in the great assembly. He judges among the gods.
WEB.2: “How long will you judge unjustly, and show partiality to the wicked?” Selah.
WEB.3: “Defend the weak, the poor, and the fatherless. Maintain the rights of the poor and oppressed.
WEB.4: Rescue the weak and needy. Deliver them out of the hand of the wicked.”
WEB.5: They don’t know, neither do they understand. They walk back and forth in darkness. All the foundations of the earth are shaken.
WEB.6: I said, “You are gods, all of you are sons of the Most High.
WEB.7: Nevertheless you shall die like men, and fall like one of the rulers.”
WEB.8: Arise, God, judge the earth, for you inherit all of the nations.

AMP.1:
A Psalm of Asaph. God stands in the divine assembly;
He judges among the gods (divine beings).
AMP.2:
How long will you judge unjustly
And show partiality to the wicked? Selah.
AMP.3:
Vindicate the weak and fatherless;
Do justice and maintain the rights of the afflicted and destitute.
AMP.4:
Rescue the weak and needy;
Rescue them from the hand of the wicked.
AMP.5:
The rulers do not know nor do they understand;
They walk on in the darkness [of complacent satisfaction];
All the foundations of the earth [the fundamental principles of the administration of justice] are shaken.
AMP.6:
I said, "You are gods;
Indeed, all of you are sons of the Most High.
AMP.7:
"Nevertheless you will die like men
And fall like any one of the princes."
AMP.8:
Arise, O God, judge the earth!
For to You belong all the nations.

MSG.1:
An Asaph psalm
God calls the judges into his courtroom,
he puts all the judges in the dock.
MSG.2:
"Enough! You've corrupted justice long enough,
you've let the wicked get away with murder.
MSG.3:
You're here to defend the defenseless,
to make sure that underdogs get a fair break;
MSG.4:
Your job is to stand up for the powerless,
and prosecute all those who exploit them."
MSG.5:
Ignorant judges! Head-in-the-sand judges!
They haven't a clue to what's going on.
And now everything's falling apart,
the world's coming unglued.
MSG.6:
"I commissioned you judges, each one of you,
deputies of the High God,
MSG.7:
But you've betrayed your commission
and now you're stripped of your rank, busted."
MSG.8:
O God, give them their just deserts!
You've got the whole world in your hands!

NIV.1:
Psalm 82
A psalm of Asaph.
God presides in the great assembly;
he gives judgment among the “gods”:
NIV.2:
“How long will you defend the unjust
and show partiality to the wicked? Selah
NIV.3:
Defend the cause of the weak and fatherless;
maintain the rights of the poor and oppressed.
NIV.4:
Rescue the weak and needy;
deliver them from the hand of the wicked.
NIV.5:
“They know nothing, they understand nothing.
They walk about in darkness;
all the foundations of the earth are shaken.
NIV.6:
“I said, ‘You are “gods”;
you are all sons of the Most High.’
NIV.7:
But you will die like mere men;
you will fall like every other ruler.”
NIV.8:
Rise up, O God, judge the earth,
for all the nations are your inheritance.

NRSVCE.1:
God has taken his place in the divine council;
in the midst of the gods he holds judgment:
NRSVCE.2:
“How long will you judge unjustly
and show partiality to the wicked? Selah
NRSVCE.3:
Give justice to the weak and the orphan;
maintain the right of the lowly and the destitute.
NRSVCE.4:
Rescue the weak and the needy;
deliver them from the hand of the wicked.”
NRSVCE.5:
They have neither knowledge nor understanding,
they walk around in darkness;
all the foundations of the earth are shaken.
NRSVCE.6:
I say, “You are gods,
children of the Most High, all of you;
NRSVCE.7:
nevertheless, you shall die like mortals,
and fall like any prince.”
NRSVCE.8:
Rise up, O God, judge the earth;
for all the nations belong to you!

NET.1: God stands in the assembly of El; in the midst of the gods he renders judgment.
NET.2: He says, “How long will you make unjust legal decisions and show favoritism to the wicked? (Selah)
NET.3: Defend the cause of the poor and the fatherless! Vindicate the oppressed and suffering!
NET.4: Rescue the poor and needy! Deliver them from the power of the wicked!
NET.5: They neither know nor understand. They stumble around in the dark, while all the foundations of the earth crumble.
NET.6: I thought, ‘You are gods; all of you are sons of the Most High.’
NET.7: Yet you will die like mortals; you will fall like all the other rulers.”
NET.8: Rise up, O God, and execute judgment on the earth! For you own all the nations.
